sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.66 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: A Schmidt$(p, q)$-group is a finite non-nilpotent group all of whose proper subgroups are nilpotent and whose Sylow$p$-subgroup is normal. The$N$-critical graph$_{Nc}(G)$of a finite group$G$is a directed graph on the vertex set of all prime divisors of$|G|$in which$(p, q)$is an edge of$_{Nc}(G)$if and only if$G$has a Schmidt$(p, q)$-subgroup. Suppose that a finite group$G$is such that$G=AB=AC=BC$,...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
